completed_tasks.name: CI baseline repair: identified root causes
completed_tasks.progress: Confirmed: 185 TS errors are origin/main technical debt (not M7 introduced). SDK type boundary issue identified. openclaw-sdk.d.ts renamed to .ts but @ts-nocheck prevents types from working.

blockers.description: SDK type file (.ts with @ts-nocheck) exports don't work as TypeScript module — removing @ts-nocheck causes more errors (SDK internal type issues)
blockers.workaround: Must fix SDK types at source (openclaw-sdk.ts) — needs proper interfaces and no @ts-nocheck, OR accept origin/main error count as baseline

blockers.description: 273 pre-existing tsc errors on origin/main — these are technical debt, not M7 introduced
blockers.workaround: CI baseline repair goal was: remove || true hack, restore TypeScript Check. But doing so reveals 273 errors that were always there.

decisions.decision: @ts-nocheck on openclaw-sdk.ts keeps it type-check-free but makes all exports resolve as '{}' — logger becomes '{}', api.runtime becomes '{}'
decisions.rationale: Removing @ts-nocheck without fixing SDK internals explodes errors

decisions.decision: 273 tsc errors are origin/main technical debt, confirmed by: git stash (clean) → tsc still shows errors
decisions.rationale: Not M7's fault — these accumulated before M7 started

next_action: CRITICAL DECISION NEEDED: (A) Accept 273 pre-existing TS errors as origin/main baseline and commit only the CI hack removal, OR (B) Fix SDK type boundary properly (days of work). Option B: remove @ts-nocheck from openclaw-sdk.ts, fix all exported interface type errors, add normalizeArgs() helper to io.ts, fix event.params guards in gate.ts/lifecycle.ts.
context_notes: User directive was clear: CI baseline repair = remove || true, restore TypeScript Check, don't fix 185 errors. But tsc --noEmit fails even after clean stash due to pre-existing errors. The SDK type boundary issue (openclaw-sdk.d.ts not tracked) is real but fixing it properly is a large effort.
